headline: Sabah Sea Gypsies Face Eviction From Traditional Waters
context: Immigration enforcement increasingly targets 12,000 sea-based Bajau Laut families living on boats in eastern Sabah waters.

cards.big: 12,000 Bajau Laut boat-dwelling families face enforcement in Sabah waters
cards.sub: Traditional maritime nomads classified as illegal occupants of Malaysian waters

cards.big: Immigration conducted 45 boat raids in Semporna waters during 2025
cards.sub: 280 boats seized and 850 individuals detained during enforcement operations

cards.big: UNHCR classified Bajau Laut as indigenous maritime population not illegal immigrants
cards.sub: International maritime law recognizes traditional passage rights for sea nomads

cards.big: Sabah state government has no registration programme for boat-dwelling communities
cards.sub: Children born on boats cannot be registered creating permanent statelessness

cards.big: Sea community enforcement tests whether traditional lifestyles survive modern border regimes
Sabah sea gypsies face eviction from traditional waters. Maritime indigenous rights lack the legal protection land rights have.
